mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale
rationale: status: open
reason: targeted verification uncovered a pre-existing managed-process shutdown failure in cli/internal/server that blocks the staged package gate. cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown -count=1 passes, but cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run TestServerShutdownCallsWorkerManagerShutdown -count=1 and cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees -count=1 both fail with "process pid ... still alive after cleanup" after Shutdown completes. The new bounded supervisor wait change is in place, but I cannot truthfully commit red code while the package gate fails.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Atomic child slice (part 1 of 3) with file:line root cause evidence (server.go:379-385, workers.go:1696-1703, server_service.go:26-42), a concrete proposed fix (bounded grace wait before falling through to worker cleanup), explicit NON-SCOPE, and a single numbered AC naming a specific test (TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown). Executable as written. Four of five prior attempts failed for infra reasons unrelated to bead content (three 'session closed before Stop hook', one no-commit-evidence). The most recent attempt shows the required test itself passing after the change was implemented; it stalled only because the agent judged the change couldn't be committed while two out-of-scope sibling-package tests (managed-process cleanup) were also red, which is a pre-existing issue outside this child's NON-SCOPE, not a defect in this bead. Not escalating to operator_required: the bead's own AC does not literally demand a full-package-green gate, decision_audit marked that attempt 'recoverable'/'retry', and no ambiguity blocks starting an implementation attempt.","fingerprint":"0442022a12b3c1175c1c264d7abd7f7b51b741c36312fe93ed0f1adbab8b067c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"0442022a12b3c1175c1c264d7abd7f7b51b741c36312fe93ed0f1adbab8b067c"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"package-wide gate blocked by pre-existing, out-of-scope test failures: The AC itself is narrow and already proven satisfiable in isolation (prior_attempts[2] reports TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown passes standalone), so this is not a decomposition or ambiguity problem. But that same attempt hit a hidden external blocker: the package-wide gate fails on two pre-existing, out-of-scope tests (TestServerShutdownCallsWorkerManagerShutdown, TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees) with 'process pid ... still alive after cleanup', which the repo's commit/lefthook gate would also trip on. Nothing in the bead or subsequent notes states this pre-existing failure was cleared — the two more-recent prior_attempts (PTY closed before Stop hook) are infra flakes that never reached test execution, so they neither confirm nor refute the blocker. A narrow AC rewrite can't fix a repo-wide test/commit gate failure, so this needs operator triage (fix or explicitly waive the pre-existing failure) before dispatch.","fingerprint":"fe488f769cc416f5317728929bf19bb6607ce46bf4e05403f2c96f2110526270","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"fe488f769cc416f5317728929bf19bb6607ce46bf4e05403f2c96f2110526270"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"a non-stale prior_attempt shows the named test passes in isolation but the package-wide gate fails on pre-existing, out-of-scope sibling test failures, and no subsequent note/reopen clears this: The bead is a well-formed decomposition slice: root cause is pinned to server.go:379-385, workers.go:1696-1703, and server_service.go:26-42, the fix approach is concrete (bounded grace wait before falling through to worker cleanup), and the single AC line names a specific test (TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown). The most recent three prior_attempts failed on Claude TUI PTY/Stop-hook infra issues unrelated to bead quality. However, the fourth (non-stale, uncleared) prior_attempt is a real signal: the agent got the named test itself passing, but the full `cli/internal/server` package gate failed due to pre-existing, out-of-scope sibling test failures (TestServerShutdownCallsWorkerManagerShutdown, TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees) tied to managed-process-tree cleanup, which this child's NON-SCOPE explicitly excludes. No later note or reopen clears that finding, so per stale-blocker precedence it must be treated as current. This creates a genuine ambiguity between the bead's own single-test AC and the general package-gate convention, which is resolvable with a narrow AC clarification rather than a structural rewrite or re-decomposition.","fingerprint":"614a53b2ac1d1ceb45cf1d762d8a6623cf3b6c60c56b1fbe0030578c19955689","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"614a53b2ac1d1ceb45cf1d762d8a6623cf3b6c60c56b1fbe0030578c19955689"}
